Part B.key_evidence: Single detection (ndethist=1) at a position covered 1397 times previously with no prior detections, indicating a one-off transient event. The source appears point-like (classtar=0.93, fwhm=2.15 px) in the science image with a clear positive residual in the difference. No PS1 counterpart is close by (distpsnr1≈21"), and there is no visible host galaxy at the candidate position in the reference cutout. isdiffpos=t confirms brightening in science.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Most consistent with a solar-system object (asteroid): a single, isolated, point-source positive detection with no underlying host or prior activity at a well-sampled field position is the classic asteroid signature. The lack of any nearby PS1 galaxy/star match and the clean point-like morphology support a moving object caught on one epoch.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A hostless supernova (or SN far from its faint host) cannot be strictly excluded from a single epoch, and an AGN/variable star flare in an uncatalogued faint source is conceivable, but the complete absence of any reference-image counterpart or nearby PS1 source, combined with the very high ncovhist/ndethist ratio, makes a stationary astrophysical transient less likely than a moving body.
